elements.text: enclosure classification. To make this determination, each building wall shall be assumed as the windward wall for consideration of the amount of openings present with respect to the remaining building envelope.

parameters: R_n = power spectral density of turbulence at equivalent height
N_1 = natural reduced frequency

elements.text: 26.12.3 Protection of Glazed Openings
Glazed openings in Risk Category II, III, or IV buildings located in hurricane-prone regions shall be protected as specified in this section.
N_1 = \frac{n_1 L_{\bar{z}}}{\bar{V}_{\bar{z}}}
parameters: N_1 = natural reduced frequency
n_1 = natural frequency
L_{\bar{z}} = turbulence integral length scale
\bar{V}_{\bar{z}} = mean hourly wind speed
L_z is defined in Equation (26.11-9). The size effect factors related to the height
breadth
and depth of the building are

elements.text: 26.12.3.1 Wind-Borne Debris Regions
Glazed openings shall be protected in accordance with Section 26.12.3.2 in the following locations:

1. Within 1 mi (1.6 km) of the mean high water line where an Exposure D condition exists upwind of the waterline and the basic wind speed is equal to or greater than 130 mi/h (58 m/s), or
2. In areas where the basic wind speed is equal to or greater than 140 mi/h (63 m/s).
R_h = \frac{1}{\eta_h} - \frac{1}{2\eta_h^2}(1 - e^{-2\eta_h})
parameters: R_h = size effect factor related to height
\eta_h = turbulent coherence factor in height direction

elements.text: R_B = \frac{1}{\eta_B} - \frac{1}{2\eta_B^2}(1 - e^{-2\eta_B})
parameters: R_B = size effect factor related to breadth
\eta_B = turbulent coherence factor in breadth direction

elements.text: For Risk Category II buildings and other structures and Risk Category III buildings and other structures, except health care facilities, the wind-borne debris region shall be based on Figure 26.5-1B. For Risk Category III health care facilities, the wind-borne debris region shall be based on Figure 26.5-1C. For Risk Category IV buildings and other structures, the wind-borne debris region shall be based on Figure 26.5-1D. Risk categories shall be determined in accordance with Section 1.5.
R_L = \frac{1}{\eta_L} - \frac{1}{2\eta_L^2}(1 - e^{-2\eta_L})
parameters: R_L = size effect factor related to depth
\eta_L = turbulent coherence factor in depth direction
the turbulent coherence (correlation) factors in the corresponding directions
evaluated at the natural reduced frequency

elements.text: **EXCEPTION:** Glazing located more than 60 ft (18.3 m) above the ground and more than 30 ft (9.2 m) above aggregate-surfaced roofs, including roofs with gravel or stone ballast, located within 1,500 ft (458 m) of the building shall be permitted to be unprotected.

parameters: \eta_h = turbulent coherence factor in height direction
\eta_B = turbulent coherence factor in breadth direction
\eta_L = turbulent coherence factor in depth direction
n_1 = natural frequency
h = height of structure
B = breadth of structure
L = depth of structure
\bar{V}_{\bar{z}} = mean hourly wind speed

elements.text: 26.12.3.2 Protection Requirements for Glazed Openings

Glazing in buildings requiring protection shall be impact resistant or be protected with an impact-protective system. Impact-protective systems and impact-resistant glazing shall be subjected to missile test and cyclic pressure differential tests in accordance with ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996 as applicable. Testing to demonstrate compliance with ASTM E1996 shall be in accordance with ASTM E1886. Impact-resistant glazing and impact-protective systems shall comply with the missile criteria of Section 5 of ASTM E1996 based on the missile required by Table 3 or Table 4 of ASTM E1996. Glazing in residential doors, rolling doors, flexible doors shall be subjected to missile tests and cyclic pressure differential tests in accordance with ANSI/DASMA 115 as applicable.
The mean hourly wind speed (in ft/s or m/s) at the equivalent structure height, \bar{z}, is
\bar{V}_{\bar{z}} = \bar{b}\left(\frac{\bar{z}}{33}\right)^{\bar{\alpha}}\frac{88}{60}V
parameters: \bar{V}_{\bar{z}} = mean hourly wind speed at equivalent structure height
\bar{b} = constant from Table 26.11-1
\bar{z} = equivalent structure height
\bar{\alpha} = constant from Table 26.11-1
V = basic wind speed
combined gust-effect factors and pressure coefficients (GC_p), (GC_pi)
and (GC_pf) are given in figures and tables
the gust-effect factor shall not be determined separately.
\bar{b} and \bar{\alpha} are constants listed in Table 26.11-1, \bar{z} is obtained from Section 26.11.4
and V is the basic wind speed
mi/h (m/s).

elements.text: Glazing and impact-protective systems in buildings and other structures classified as Risk Category IV in accordance with Section 1.5 shall comply with the "enhanced protection" requirements of Table 3 of ASTM E1996. Glazing and impact-protective systems in all other buildings covered by Section 26.12.3.1 shall comply with the "basic protection" requirements of Table 3 of ASTM E1996.
26.11.6 Rational Analysis
In lieu of the procedure defined in Sections 26.11.4 and 26.11.5, determination of the gust-effect factor by any rational analysis defined in the recognized literature is permitted.
**EXCEPTION:** When approved, other testing methods and/or performance criteria shall be permitted to be used to demonstrate compliance with all provisions of this section.
26.11.7 Limitations
**26.13 INTERNAL PRESSURE COEFFICIENTS**
Internal pressure coefficients (GC_{pi}) shall be determined from Table 26.13-1 based on building enclosure classifications determined from Section 26.12.
**26.12 ENCLOSURE CLASSIFICATION**
26.13.1 Reduction Factor for Large-Volume Buildings, R_i
26.12.1 General
For a partially enclosed building containing a single,

For the purpose of determining internal pressure coefficients, buildings and other structures for which internal pressure coefficients (GC_pi) apply shall be classified as enclosed, partially enclosed, partially open, or open, as defined in Section 26.2. If a building or other structure satisfies both the "open" and "partially enclosed" enclosure classification definition, it shall be classified as a "partially open" building or other structure.
26.12.2 Openings

A determination shall be made of the amount of openings in the building envelope for use in determining the enclosure classification. To make this determination, each building wall shall be assumed as the windward wall for consideration of the amount of openings present with respect to the remaining building envelope.
